value: Secure Boot 2011 certificate expiry posture case - not a live intrusion; CVEs and certificate names are the primary IOCs and historical bootkit hashes are labeled as class exemplars not fresh artifacts
context: Read before triaging - frozen-DBX exposure window opens 2026-06-24 (KEK CA 2011 expiry)

value: Microsoft Corporation KEK CA 2011 expires 2026-06-24; Microsoft Corporation UEFI CA 2011 expires 2026-06-27; Microsoft Windows Production PCA 2011 expires 2026-10-19 (Microsoft official table updated 2026-05-18)
context: The forcing event - after KEK expiry a non-migrated device can never receive a new DB/DBX entry
Expiring Key Exchange Key (June 24 2026) - presence in KEK store after expiry means the revocation channel is frozen
Expiring DB certificate (June 27 2026) - signs Linux shim and third-party option ROMs
Signs the Windows Boot Manager bootmgfw.efi - expires 2026-10-19
Replacement KEK - its presence confirms the device migrated and dbx can still update
Replacement DB cert for OS bootloaders (split from monolithic 2011)
Replacement DB cert for hardware option ROMs (split from monolithic 2011)
Replacement signer for the Windows Boot Manager
DWORD 0x5944 opt-in that authorizes Windows Update to perform the managed certificate transition
OS-reported Secure Boot enable flag - not ground truth on its own
Windows Boot Manager on the ESP - BlackLotus replaces it with a signed-but-revoked older version
Fallback boot path on the ESP - alternate bootkit staging location
GRUB2 bootloader affected by BootHole CVE-2020-10713
Linux shim signed by UEFI CA 2011 - stops verifying once 2011 cert removed without a 2023-signed shim
Microsoft recovery binary that re-adds the Windows UEFI CA 2023 cert if cert state is wiped
UEFI Security Architectural Protocol pointer - NULLed by the Framework Bombshell signed UEFI shell mm command to disable LoadImage signature checks
BlackLotus Secure Boot bypass in Windows Boot Manager - patched May 2023 but requires manual DBX revocation
BootHole - GRUB2 buffer overflow affecting virtually all Secure Boot Linux and Windows trusting third-party UEFI components
Howyar SysReturn reloader.efi - Microsoft-signed loader that runs unsigned payload from a hardcoded path; revoked Jan 2025 Patch Tuesday
Signed BIOS-flashing utility for rugged tablets executes on any Secure Boot platform; 14 dbx signatures added 2025-06-10
PKfail - leaked AMI test Platform Keys marked DO NOT TRUST shipped in 800+ device models

value: Eradication of a confirmed bootkit requires rebuilding the ESP from known-good media AND re-flashing OEM firmware - a standard OS reimage does not remove a below-OS implant
context: Do not just reimage

value: Apply OEM BIOS firmware update BEFORE the Windows Update certificate change so the 2023 certs are embedded in firmware and survive a CMOS clear or board RMA
context: Sequencing is the single most important decision in the rollout
